name: Langkit_Support.Vectors

documentation.description:
.. note:: This unit is internal: only Langkit and Langkit-generated
libraries are supposed to use it.

This package implements a very simple Vector type. It has the following
attributes:

- Very lightweight implementation, very few primitives.
- Not controlled (manual memory management).
- Ada 2012-like iteration via the Iterate aspect, so read-only access to
  elements in for .. of loops.
- Uses realloc for resize, so faster, but won't be correct on every type.
- Not tagged, so no dot notation on primitives.
- Small vector optimization: can store a number of the elements inline.

array_types.name: Elements_Array
array_types.code: type Elements_Array is array (Index_Type range <>) of Element_Type;

tagged_types.name: Vector
tagged_types.code:
type Vector is tagged private
  with Iterable =>
    (First       => First_Index,
     Next        => Next,
     Has_Element => Has_Element,
     Element     => Get);

access_types.name: Element_Access
access_types.code: type Element_Access is not null access all Element_Type;

documentation.description:
Like Index_Type, but also covers zero, which is used to represent a
dummy last index value for empty vectors.

subprograms.name: Append
subprograms.code: procedure Append (Self : in out Vector; Element : Element_Type)

documentation.description: Appends Element to Self

subprograms.name: Clear
subprograms.code: procedure Clear (Self : in out Vector)

documentation.description:
Remove every element in this vector.
NOTICE: this function does not actually free the memory of the vector!

subprograms.name: Concat
subprograms.code: procedure Concat (Self : in out Vector; Elements : Elements_Array)

documentation.description: Append every element of the Elements array in Self

subprograms.name: Concat
subprograms.code: procedure Concat (Self : in out Vector; Elements : Vector)

documentation.description: Append every element of the Elements array in Self

subprograms.name: Copy
subprograms.code: function Copy (Self : Vector) return Vector

documentation.description: Return newly allocated copy of Self

subprograms.name: Cut
subprograms.code: procedure Cut (Self : in out Vector; Index : Iteration_Index_Type)

documentation.description: Remove all elements after ``Index`` in ``Self``

subprograms.name: Destroy
subprograms.code: procedure Destroy (Self : in out Vector)

documentation.description: Destroy this vector

subprograms.name: First_Element
subprograms.code: function First_Element (Self : Vector) return Element_Type

documentation.description: Return the first element in this vector

subprograms.name: First_Index
subprograms.code: function First_Index (Self : Vector) return Iteration_Index_Type

documentation.description: Return the first index, only used for the Iterable aspect

subprograms.name: Generic_Sort

documentation.description: Sort elements in Self so that Self.Element (1) < Self.Element (2) < ...

subprograms.name: Get
subprograms.code:
function Get
  (Self : Vector; Index : Iteration_Index_Type) return Element_Type

documentation.description: Get the element at Index

subprograms.name: Get_Access
subprograms.code:
function Get_Access
  (Self : Vector; Index : Index_Type)
   return Element_Access

documentation.description:
Get an access to the element at Index
NOTICE: This access is unsafe, and might get invalidated if the Vector
is reallocated. Hence, its lifetime is considered to be as long as the
vector is not modified.

subprograms.name: Has_Element
subprograms.code:
function Has_Element
  (Self : Vector; N : Iteration_Index_Type) return Boolean

documentation.description:
Given a vector and an index, return True if the index is in the vector
range. Only used for the iterable aspect.

subprograms.name: Image

documentation.description:
Generic function, that, given a Image function on the element type,
returning the representation of an instance of Element_Type as a string,
will return a string representation of the vector.

subprograms.name: Is_Empty
subprograms.code: function Is_Empty (Self : Vector) return Boolean

documentation.description: Return whether Self is an empty vector

subprograms.name: Last_Element
subprograms.code: function Last_Element (Self : Vector) return Element_Access

documentation.description:
Return an access to the last element in this vector
NOTICE: Read Get_Access's documentation.

subprograms.name: Last_Element
subprograms.code: function Last_Element (Self : Vector) return Element_Type

documentation.description: Return the last element in this vector

subprograms.name: Last_Index
subprograms.code: function Last_Index (Self : Vector) return Iteration_Index_Type

documentation.description:
Return the index of the last element in this vector or
First_Index (Self) - 1 if this vector is empty.

subprograms.name: Length
subprograms.code: function Length (Self : Vector) return Natural

documentation.description: Return the Length of the vector, ie. the number of elements it contains

subprograms.name: Next
subprograms.code:
function Next
  (Self : Vector; N : Iteration_Index_Type) return Iteration_Index_Type

documentation.description:
Given a vector and an index, return the next index. Only used for the
iterable aspect.

subprograms.name: Pop
subprograms.code: function Pop (Self : in out Vector) return Element_Type

documentation.description: Pop the last element from vector and return it

subprograms.name: Pop
subprograms.code: function Pop (Self : in out Vector; N : Index_Type) return Element_Type

documentation.description:
Move the last element in Self at index N and then remove the last
element. This makes it possible to remove an item from the vector while
iterating on it.

subprograms.name: Pop
subprograms.code: procedure Pop (Self : in out Vector)

documentation.description: Pop the last element from vector

subprograms.name: Pop
subprograms.code: procedure Pop (Self : in out Vector; N : Index_Type)

documentation.description:
Move the last element in Self at index N and then remove the last
element. This makes it possible to remove an item from the vector while
iterating on it.

subprograms.name: Previous
subprograms.code:
function Previous
  (Self : Vector; N : Iteration_Index_Type) return Iteration_Index_Type

documentation.description:
Given a vector and an index, return the next index. Only used for the
iterable aspect.

subprograms.name: Remove_At
subprograms.code:
procedure Remove_At
  (Self : in out Vector; Index : Index_Type; Count : Positive := 1)

subprograms.name: Reserve
subprograms.code: procedure Reserve (Self : in out Vector; Capacity : Natural)

documentation.description:
Make sure that Self has enough room to contain Capacity elements in
total.

subprograms.name: Set
subprograms.code: procedure Set (Self : in out Vector; Index : Index_Type; E : Element_Type)

documentation.description: Set the element at Index to E

subprograms.name: Slice
subprograms.code:
function Slice
  (Self : Vector; First, Last : Natural) return Elements_Array

documentation.description: Return a (shallow) copy of Self (First .. Last) as an array

subprograms.name: To_Array
subprograms.code:
function To_Array
  (Self : Vector) return Elements_Array

documentation.description: Return a (shallow) copy of the current vector as an array
